title: echinocereus fendleri subsp. rectispinus
intro: Echinocereus fendleri subsp. rectispinus, a rare and lesser-known cactus species, is a type of succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds and prevent infection. The health benefits of Echinocereus fendleri subsp. rectispinus include its ability to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as arthritis, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and powdering the stems, which can then be used to make teas, salves, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
